title: Crisp Vietnamese Chicken Noodles
description: A rich and crisp Vietnamese dish featuring amaretti and miso-marinated chicken, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine miso paste, soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, grated ginger, and garlic, and blend until smooth.
In a shallow dish, whisk together the miso mixture and 2 tablespoons of water.
Add the chicken to the marinade and toss to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to 2 hours.
Remove the chicken from the marinade and season with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook for 5-6 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Transfer the chicken to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and cook for 1 minute.
Add the noodles to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until slightly browned and crispy.
Add the chopped green onions and cilantro to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Slice the cooked chicken into thin strips and add to the skillet with the noodles and herbs.
Stir in the crushed amaretti cookies and cook for 1 minute, until the cookies are lightly toasted.
Serve the chicken and noodle mixture hot, garnished with additional green onions and cilantro if desired.
